compatibilityLabel: scheduler 0.27.0 public root entrypoint behavior oracle

generation.method: exact scheduler npm tarball plus local scheduler implementation copied under an isolated alias into a temporary node_modules tree
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per target, scenario, and mode
generation.timingNormalization: raw wall-clock timestamps are omitted; probes record logical ordering, timeout buckets, and boolean didTimeout categories; local package metadata is observed but omitted from behavior comparison

afterWorker164.source: packages/scheduler/index.js and cjs/scheduler.* are copied under fast-react-scheduler for root-entry behavior comparison

probeModes.reason: The public scheduler root entrypoint selects cjs/scheduler.development.js when NODE_ENV is not production.
The public scheduler root entrypoint selects cjs/scheduler.production.js when NODE_ENV is production.

scenarios.area: Scheduled task object shape and timeout categories

captures: ready callback execution order across all public priorities
current priority level while each callback executes
current priority level for FIFO callbacks
ready work runs before delayed work
delayed task runs after becoming eligible
callback return function is stored as a continuation
continuation runs before later same-priority work
continuation keeps the same current priority level
UserBlocking callbacks receive didTimeout true after a blocked event loop
Normal callbacks remain non-timeout under the same generous block
wrapCallback this, argument, and return forwarding
requestPaint resets on the next host callback
setImmediate is captured before requiring scheduler
ready work is scheduled through setImmediate in Node
transport evidence is recorded without temp paths

fastReactScheduler.source: local packages/scheduler copied for behavior comparison

implementationRisks: The local scheduler package metadata intentionally remains workspace-specific and is excluded from root scheduler behavior comparisons.

The oracle intentionally covers the public scheduler root entrypoint only; mock scheduler, post-task scheduler, native scheduler, reconciler lane scheduling, and renderer behavior remain separate compatibility surfaces.
Timing assertions are restricted to normalized logical values and timeout buckets, not wall-clock latency.
timingCaveats: The checked artifact intentionally omits raw times and absolute timestamps.
The didTimeout scenario blocks the event loop for at least 400ms, comfortably beyond UserBlocking's 250ms timeout and far below Normal's 5000ms timeout.
Delayed callback probes assert execution order and cancellation behavior, not precise millisecond delivery.
